evals.name: Greenfield SPA scaffold completeness
evals.prompt: Scaffold a React app for a SaaS project management tool. Team of 5, TypeScript required, deploy to Vercel, REST API backend. Use Vite, React Router v7, Zustand, TanStack Query, Tailwind, Vitest, Playwright.
evals.expected_output: Full scaffold with feature-first structure, all config files, CI pipeline, env validation

assertions.text: Output includes vite.config.ts with path aliases
Output includes tsconfig.json with strict: true
Output includes src/env.ts with Zod validation
Output includes package.json with vitest, @testing-library/react, playwright
Output includes GitHub Actions CI workflow file
Output includes src/test/setup.ts with MSW configuration
Output includes feature-first folder structure (src/features/)
Output does NOT reference create-react-app or react-scripts
Output includes ADR documenting stack decisions
Output includes Dockerfile with multi-stage build

evals.name: CRA migration to Vite
evals.prompt: I have a Create React App project with React 17 and JavaScript. Help me migrate to Vite 5 + TypeScript incrementally. Team has mixed TS experience.
evals.expected_output: Migration guide with incremental TypeScript adoption, new tsconfig with allowJs, vite.config, migration checklist

assertions.text: Output addresses CRA deprecation with evidence
Output provides tsconfig.json with allowJs: true for incremental migration
Output provides vite.config.ts that handles CommonJS imports
Output provides a migration checklist with ordered steps
Output does NOT suggest enabling strict: true immediately (incremental approach)
Output documents React Router v5 to v7 breaking changes

evals.name: Config audit on existing scaffold
evals.prompt: Audit this React project configuration: tsconfig has strict: false, .env file is not in .gitignore, vite.config has no test config, no ESLint setup.
evals.expected_output: Config audit report with [PASS], [WARN], [FAIL] tags and specific fixes for each failure

assertions.text: Output flags strict: false as [FAIL] or [WARN]
Output flags missing .gitignore .env entry as [FAIL]
Output flags missing ESLint setup as [FAIL] or [WARN]
Output provides specific fix for each failure, not just description
Output uses [PASS], [WARN], [FAIL] classification consistently

evals.name: Testing infrastructure setup
evals.prompt: Set up the complete testing infrastructure for a React app: Vitest unit tests with RTL, MSW for API mocking, and Playwright E2E. Include example tests for a LoginForm component.
evals.expected_output: Complete test setup: setup.ts, handlers.ts, render-utils.tsx, LoginForm test with 3 scenarios, playwright.config.ts, smoke E2E test

assertions.text: Output includes src/test/setup.ts with jest-dom import and MSW hooks
Output includes MSW handlers.ts with at least one example handler
Output includes custom render function wrapping QueryClientProvider
Output uses userEvent.setup() not fireEvent
Output uses findBy* for async assertions, not waitFor + getBy
Output includes playwright.config.ts with baseURL and browser projects
Output includes at least one smoke E2E test
Output does NOT use getByTestId as primary query

evals.name: Performance-critical SPA with budget constraints
evals.prompt: Scaffold a React SPA with 120KB gzipped bundle budget, LCP <2.5s target, manual chunk splitting, and module federation support. No SSR available.
evals.expected_output: vite.config with manualChunks, React.lazy usage, performance-oriented config, rollup-plugin-visualizer

assertions.text: Output includes manualChunks configuration in vite.config
Output uses React.lazy + Suspense for route splitting
Output includes rollup-plugin-visualizer for bundle analysis
Output does NOT recommend SSR (constraint is SPA-only)
Output documents the chunk splitting strategy with rationale
Output references TanStack Query staleTime for caching strategy
